citations.author: Shakib Jahshan
citations.sentence: O beautiful one of Safad

citations.author: Nuh Ibrahim
citations.sentence: From Akka's Prison came the funerals, Mohammad Jamjoum and Fouad Hejazi,

citations.author: Mahmoud Darwish
citations.sentence:
I may add to the story a description of Acre,
the oldest beautiful cities,
the most beautiful of the ancient cities.

citations.author: Rashed Hussain
citations.sentence:
Have you seen your wall, mocking the sea, indifferent to its love,
until you went out to it to listen to the stirrings of its heart?
Or did you reject its plea, and go out in revolt to wage war against it?

citations.author: Anonymous
citations.sentence:
They promised I could smell the same hot bread,
eat fresh thyme from under the window,
breathe the breeze of Haifa,
but…
but… that was a false promise.

citations.author: Mohammed al-Qudwa
citations.sentence:
I miss Haifa.
I comb with my eyes
Sea foam for the last time

citations.author: Mahmoud Darwish
citations.sentence:
I love the seas that I will love,
I love the fields that I will love,
but a drop of water on the feather of a lark
among the stones of Haifa
equals all the seas
and washes me of the sins I will commit.

citations.author: Kamal Ibraheem
citations.sentence:
Tiberias, city of our forebears,
your covenant reaches back to Herod, before the birth of Christ.
Your history is a song still rising,
a lament of a Nakba
that has washed over you
for decades without end.

citations.author: Motalaq Abed El Khalek
citations.sentence:
O memory of Tiberias —
there dwell within you maidens,
playful with hearts.

citations.author: Suliman Al-Mashini
citations.sentence:
Peace be upon the town of Nazareth,
peace upon her radiant, flourishing grove.
Peace — and a thousand peaces — upon
the proud bride of our cities.

citations.author: Taha Muhammed
citations.sentence:
Already birds are flying into your garden,
Lark and quail, sand in their wings.
The garden is in front, the desert is not far.
Somewhere a bus is burning.

citations.author: Ahmad Mefleh
citations.sentence:
Is there still in your eyes a summer and a star?
Is there still between your shores a quicksilver tide?
Take me — you have already set all my vessels aflame,
and inscribed my verses upon your green waist.

citations.author: Anonymous
citations.sentence:
Stand. Resist.
For in the rubble there are stones — countless stones,
and in the scent of blood, messages of glad tidings.
It is a day — or a battle —
but there are a thousand thousand days still to come.
Their name is Jenin.

citations.author: Etel Adnan
citations.sentence:
But they came, the bastards, to eradicate,
with bombs,
to tell very simply that we didn’t exist.
They started with the olive trees,

citations.author: Shady Abu Kheit
citations.sentence:
Tulkarm lives in my heart forever — I do not forget her, through the passing of time.
How could I forget her? Does one ever forget a homeland?
So keep her safe, O my Lord — keep Tulkarm.

citations.author: Mosab Abu Toha
citations.work: Ibrahim Abu Lughod and brother in Yaffa
citations.sentence:
The two walk toward the beach,
barefoot.
With his soft
index finger,
Ibrahim starts to draw
a map
of what
used to be
their home.

citations.author: Lea Haddad
citations.sentence:
all my life,
my grandmother has talked 
about the scent
of the oranges in Jaffa.
you know you’re in Jaffa 
when you can smell them, 
she tells me.

citations.author: Mohammed Al-Jowharee
citations.sentence:
In Jaffa — the day the caravans set down their loads —
a cloudburst fell, and the sky grew dark.
Night wrapped the graceful beauty,
its steps uncertain, without a guiding star.
And the streaming rain covered her
with kisses upon kisses.

citations.author: Mazen Khouwyre
citations.sentence:
Nablus, you are the sister of Jerusalem,
a full moon lit by the smile of the sun.
O haven of knowledge and companionship,
O forge of glory and valor —
your two mountains were shaped in loftiness and pride.

citations.author: Fady Joudah
citations.work: Then My Friend in Nablus Had a Dream.
citations.sentence:
Then my friend in Nablus had a dream.
The sun rose from the west
while he was praying
on a marble floor on fire.

citations.author: Mustafa Abu Sneineh
citations.sentence:
It was a wordless confession.
I recall the sun sliding into the darkness of the settlement,
Soldiers with guns beating the sunset
until it passed out.

citations.author: Tawfiq Zayyad
citations.sentence:
a generation of revolutionaries
after generation
as if we were twenty impossibilities.
In Lydda, Ramla, and the Galilee,
We are here to stay.
So drink the sea.

citations.author: Salah Waheedi
citations.sentence:
Between the old companions of Ramla and the first rain,
a scent returns from there —
memories from my childhood without limit,
impossible even to describe.

citations.author: Bei Dao
citations.sentence:
in Ramallah
the ancients play chess in the starry sky
the endgame flickers
a bird locked in a clock
jumps out to tell the time

citations.author: Ahmad Bkheit
citations.sentence:
Take another glimpse — grant me your glance once more,
so that I do not die… so that I may still see Ramallah.
My heart, as Christ once said to Mary,
and as the palm trunk longed for Mary —
the farmer of this land… my lifetime is my wheat.

citations.author: Mahmoud Darwish
citations.sentence:
In Jerusalem, and I mean within the ancient walls,
I walk from one epoch to another without a memory
to guide me.

citations.author: Nizar Qabbani
citations.sentence:
Oh Jerusalem, the fragrance of prophets
The shortest path between earth and sky
Oh Jerusalem, the citadel of laws
A beautiful child with fingers charred
and downcast eyes

citations.author: Najwan Darwish
citations.sentence:
I name you Medusa
I name you the older sister of Sodom and Gomorrah
you the baptismal basin that still burns
When I leave you I turn to stone
When I come back I turn to stone

citations.author: Karishma Magan
citations.work: I left my soul in the streets of Hebron
citations.sentence:
I left my soul in the streets of Hebron.
Where settlers throw acid on children.
Children,
who protest the system just by 
playing soccer at the checkpoints, 
depriving the checkpoints of its 
existence
and purpose.

citations.author: Ezz Al-dean Almonasra
citations.sentence:
— O my Hebron,
grapes of free al-Khalil… do not bear fruit.
And if you must bear fruit,
be poison to the enemy —
do not bear fruit.

citations.author: Basman Aldirawi
citations.sentence: Life will spread across Gaza’s chest, and there will be a resurrection: Not a wound nor a scar on her.

citations.author: Olivia Elias
citations.work: Day 38, Nov. 14, I Didn’t See the Fall This Year
citations.sentence: I didn’t see the fall this year I didn’t see the acacia blaze  the cranes fly away only bombs & more bombs on Gaza in ruins

citations.author: Anita Barrows
citations.sentence:
The sea his father
had fished in, his grandfather.
The sea whose tides, whose eddies,
whose changing temperatures
he knew, the way he knew 
the faces of his five children.  Now
it is all just memory

citations.author: Trooper Gerardy
citations.sentence:
On an Autumn evening that seems so long ago
The war-worn Walers reached them with stately step and slow,
And the guns roared welcome, peal upon thunder peal,
The Wells of old Beersheba were held by Moslem steel.

citations.author: Najah Hussein Musa
citations.sentence:
And we were told we had no magic in our cities—
We are no Eiffel Tower—
Nor Italy’s Colosseum—
But we are the minarets next to churches.

citations.author: Kemmer Anderson
citations.sentence:
Workmen restore the Church of Nativity
Among the broken hearted of Bethlehem
Where walls define our boundaries of fear.

citations.author: Rania Marjyeh
citations.work: Bethlehem…when the earth rises with the faces of women.
citations.sentence:
In Bethlehem,
the story does not begin with the stones,
nor with the church,
nor with the narrow streets…

It begins with the women
who carry the city
as the heart carries
its final pulse —
refusing to let it fade.
